questions.question: What does display: inline-block do?
options: Creates a block element that ignores width
Creates an element that flows inline but accepts block properties
Creates an inline element with block margins
questions.explanation: Inline-block flows horizontally like inline elements but accepts width, height, margin, and padding like block elements.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about inline-block elements?
options: They always start on a new line
They respect width and height properties
questions.explanation: Unlike inline elements, inline-block elements respect width, height, margin, and padding properties.

questions.question: What is a common issue with inline-block elements?
options: They create whitespace gaps between elements
questions.explanation: HTML whitespace (spaces, newlines) between inline-block elements creates visible gaps in the layout.

questions.question: How can you remove whitespace gaps between inline-block elements?
questions.explanation: Setting font-size: 0 on the parent and restoring it on children removes whitespace gaps because spaces have 0 width.

questions.question: Which property controls the alignment of inline-block elements at different heights?
questions.explanation: vertical-align controls how inline-block elements of different heights align with each other (top, middle, bottom, baseline).

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a valid method to remove inline-block whitespace gaps?
questions.explanation: While flex can be used instead of inline-block, it doesn't 'fix' the whitespace gap - it changes the layout model entirely.

questions.question: What is the default vertical-align value for inline-block elements?
questions.explanation: The default vertical-align for inline-block elements is 'baseline', which aligns the element with the baseline of its parent.

questions.question: Compared to Flexbox, when is inline-block a better choice?
options: For simple horizontal layouts where Flexbox would be overkill
questions.explanation: Inline-block is simpler for basic horizontal layouts, but Flexbox is better for complex or responsive layouts.

questions.question: How do inline-block elements behave when the container is too narrow?
options: They wrap to the next line
questions.explanation: Inline-block elements naturally wrap to the next line when there isn't enough horizontal space, similar to text.

questions.question: Which browsers support display: inline-block?
questions.explanation: display: inline-block is supported in all major browsers, including Internet Explorer 8 and above.

questions.question: What happens when you set width and height on an element with display: inline?
questions.explanation: Inline elements ignore explicit width and height properties. This is why inline-block is useful when you need both inline flow and block sizing.

questions.question: How can you center a group of inline-block elements?
options: Set text-align: center on the parent
questions.explanation: Since inline-block elements behave like inline content, text-align: center on the parent centers them horizontally.

questions.question: What is an alternative to using font-size: 0 for removing gap between inline-block elements?
questions.explanation: HTML comments (<!-- -->) placed between inline-block elements remove the whitespace without affecting font size.

questions.question: What is a common use case for inline-block?
questions.explanation: Inline-block is commonly used for horizontal navigation bars because it allows list items to sit horizontally with full padding/click area.

questions.question: How does inline-block affect the box model?
options: Follows standard box model (respects padding, border, margin)
questions.explanation: Inline-block elements follow the standard CSS box model: padding, border, and margin are all applied and respected.

questions.question: What happens to top and bottom margins on inline elements?
questions.explanation: Inline elements ignore top and bottom margins. Only left and right margins are respected.

questions.question: Which vertical-align value aligns the element with the top of the tallest element on the line?
questions.explanation: vertical-align: top aligns the element with the top of the tallest element on the current line.

questions.question: Can inline-block elements have a background image?
options: Only if they have a fixed height
Only if display is also set to block
questions.explanation: Inline-block elements support all visual CSS properties, including background images, shadows, gradients, etc.

questions.question: What happens when you apply float to an inline-block element?
options: Both float and inline-block are applied
questions.explanation: When float is applied, the element's computed display value becomes 'block' regardless of the specified display value.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of inline-block over float for horizontal layouts?
questions.explanation: Unlike floated elements, inline-block elements don't cause parent collapse, so no clearfix is needed.
